name: Fuente Clara Trail
name_original: Parque Natural Saja-Besaya – Poza Negra y Fuente Clara desde Bárcena Mayor
description: Enjoy an easy 4.3-mile hike through Saja-Besaya Natural Park to the Black Pool and Clear Fountain, starting from Bárcena Mayor.
region_intro: Hiking around Los Tojos offers a varied landscape characterized by dense woodlands, rolling hills, and paths through traditional villages. The region is part of Cantabria, providing access to natural park areas with diverse terrain. Trails often feature historical elements and scenic viewpoints, making Los Tojos a destination for outdoor exploration. Best hiking trails around Los Tojos The most popular hiking route is Fuente Clara Trail, a 4.3 miles (6.9 km) trail that takes 1 hour 53 minutes to complete. This easy route offers a gentle walk through the local landscape. Another top favourite among local hikers is Fallen Oak of Brujilices – Jou de la Collá Cabin loop from El Andinoso, a moderate 5.1 miles (8.2 km) path. This route features varied terrain with some elevation gain, leading through forested areas. Local hikers also love the Bárcena Mayor – Old Washhouse of Bárcena Mayor loop from Parque Natural Saja-Besaya, a 2.5 miles (3.9 km) trail leading through the Saja-Besaya Natural Park, often completed in about 1 hour 6 minutes. Hiking around Los Tojos is defined by dense forests, rolling hills, and paths connecting traditional villages. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy family friendly hikes to more challenging routes with greater elevation. The routes in Los Tojos are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.6 stars from over 200 reviews. More than 1,400 hikers have used komoot to explore Los Tojos's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are available around Los Tojos?
A: Los Tojos offers a diverse network of nearly 50 hiking trails. These routes cater to various skill levels, with options ranging from easy strolls to more challenging treks through the Cantabrian landscape.

Q: Are there easy, family-friendly hiking options in Los Tojos?
A: Yes, Los Tojos is well-suited for families and beginners, with 11 easy routes available. A great option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e997033643/fuente-clara-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Fuente Clara Trail&lt;/a&gt;, an easy 6.9 km path that takes less than two hours to complete, offering a gentle introduction to the local scenery.

Q: What kind of natural attractions can I expect to see on hikes in Los Tojos?
A: The region boasts several natural highlights. You can explore the impressive &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4243290" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Fallen Oak of Brujilices&lt;/a&gt;, discover the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2573626" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Source of the Ebro River&lt;/a&gt;, or enjoy the serene &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4629697" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pool area in the Argonza River&lt;/a&gt;. Many trails also lead through beautiful beech forests.

Q: Are there any viewpoints or scenic overlooks on the trails?
A: Absolutely. Hikers can find stunning vistas, such as those from the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4566932" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;El Moral Plateau&lt;/a&gt;. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40313195" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View of Pico Tordias – El Moral Hermitage loop from Cotero Alto&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate route that specifically features panoramic views.

Q: What do other hikers say about the trails in Los Tojos?
A: The trails in Los Tojos are highly regarded by the komoot community, holding an average rating of 4.6 stars from over 230 reviews. Hikers frequently praise the region's dense forests, rolling hills, and the charm of its traditional villages.

Q: Are there any circular walks available in the Los Tojos area?
A: Yes, many routes in Los Tojos are designed as circular walks, allowing you to start and end in the same location. An example is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20501376" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Bárcena Mayor – Old Washhouse of Bárcena Mayor loop from Parque Natural Saja-Besaya&lt;/a&gt;, which is an easy 3.9 km loop.

Q: What is the best time of year to go hiking in Los Tojos?
A: Los Tojos offers hiking opportunities year-round. Spring and autumn are particularly popular for their mild temperatures and vibrant natural colors, while summer provides warmer weather ideal for longer days on the trails. Winter hiking is also possible, though conditions may vary, especially at higher elevations.

Q: Are there any challenging routes for experienced hikers?
A: Yes, for those seeking a greater challenge, Los Tojos has 8 difficult routes. These trails typically feature significant elevation gain and more rugged terrain, providing a rewarding experience for advanced hikers.

Q: Can I find routes that pass through traditional villages?
A: Many trails in Los Tojos connect or pass through traditional Cantabrian villages, offering a glimpse into local culture and architecture. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38180008" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Church of San Martín loop from Pujayo&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate route that includes cultural landmarks.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on the hiking trails?
A: The terrain around Los Tojos is varied, characterized by dense woodlands, rolling hills, and paths that traverse both natural park areas and traditional village surroundings. You'll find a mix of forest paths, open tracks, and some routes with moderate elevation changes.

Q: Are there any routes that feature historical elements?
A: Yes, the region's history is often woven into its trails. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20501376" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Bárcena Mayor – Old Washhouse of Bárcena Mayor loop from Parque Natural Saja-Besaya&lt;/a&gt; takes you past the historic Old Washhouse in the charming village of Bárcena Mayor.

Q: Are there any routes that lead to unique natural monuments?
A: Indeed. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40316029" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Fallen Oak of Brujilices – Jou de la Collá Cabin loop from El Andinoso&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate route that specifically highlights the impressive &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4243290" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Fallen Oak of Brujilices&lt;/a&gt;, a notable natural monument in the area.
tips: One of the most beautiful villages in Spain. And one of the best and cheapest places to eat.

Area of pools with crystalline waters, next to the track that goes to the Arbencia well.
It is what its name indicates. Nice access trail, recommended

faqs.question: Where can I park to access the trail?
faqs.answer: The trail starts from the charming village of Bárcena Mayor. You should find parking available within or near the village, which serves as the main access point for this route.

faqs.question: Is this hike suitable for beginners or families?
faqs.answer: Yes, this route is classified as an easy hike, making it suitable for any fitness level and all skill levels. It has minimal elevation gain and generally accessible paths, perfect for families.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on the trail?
faqs.answer: The terrain is varied but generally easy. You'll encounter a mix of unpaved paths (approximately 5.90 km) and paved sections (around 864 meters). The park's landscape features deep valleys and extensive deciduous forests, providing a green and rich environment.

faqs.question: What natural features or landmarks will I see along the way?
faqs.answer: Starting from the historic village of Bárcena Mayor, you'll cross the Bárcena Mayor Bridge over the Argonza River and pass the Santa María Spring. The trail's namesake features, the Poza Negra (Black Pool) and Fuente Clara (Clear Fountain), are key natural highlights.

faqs.question: What kind of wildlife can I expect to see?
faqs.answer: The Saja-Besaya Natural Park is rich in wildlife. You might spot deer, roe deer, wild boars, and various birds of prey. While less common, the park is also home to wolves and even brown bears. The deer rutting season is a popular time for wildlife viewing in the wider park.

faqs.question: Is this trail dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: Generally, dogs are allowed on trails within natural parks in Spain, but they should always be kept on a leash to protect wildlife and ensure the safety of other hikers. Please ensure you clean up after your pet.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to hike this route?
faqs.answer: The Saja-Besaya Natural Park is known for its vast deciduous forests. Spring and autumn are particularly beautiful, with lush greenery or vibrant fall colors. Summer offers pleasant temperatures for hiking, while winter can be serene but may require appropriate gear for colder conditions.

faqs.question: Are there any permits or entrance fees for the Saja-Besaya Natural Park?
faqs.answer: As a natural park, there are typically no entrance fees or permits required for general hiking access to the Saja-Besaya Natural Park. However, always check local regulations for any specific activities or protected zones.

faqs.question: Is this a circular trail?
faqs.answer: Yes, this route is designed as a circular walk, allowing you to return to your starting point in Bárcena Mayor without retracing your steps.

faqs.question: Does this trail connect with other hiking routes?
faqs.answer: Yes, approximately 36% of this route overlaps with sections of the Senda de la Reserva del Saja, offering potential connections to other paths within the natural park.

faqs.question: Are there facilities or services available in Bárcena Mayor?
faqs.answer: Bárcena Mayor is a beautifully preserved mountain town and a historic site. As such, you can expect to find basic services, including accommodation, bars, and restaurants, making it a convenient base before or after your hike.

waypoints.name: Bárcena Mayor Bridge over the Argonza River
